east-village.note: Union Sq is the hub — 6, L, or N/R/W get you to Midtown direct or with one easy transfer.

lower-east-side.note: F or J/M/Z run direct, but stations sit at the neighborhood's edges — count the walk.

williamsburg.note: L + transfer; L is reliable but packed. Ferry is the scenic backup, not the fast one.

east-williamsburg.note: L only — one transfer to Midtown, and the train is already full by the time it reaches you.

greenpoint.note: G train only — transfer to the E/M or 7 required, plan +10 min.

bushwick.note: L or J/M/Z plus a transfer; the deeper into Bushwick you live, the longer the ride.

astoria.note: N/W direct into Midtown — no transfer, just a steady elevated ride over the bridge.

long-island-city.note: 7 or E/M direct — Midtown is one stop away; the G covers your Brooklyn trips.

hells-kitchen.note: Walkable to the office — A/C/E or 1 only matter on rainy days.

yorkville.note: Q direct or 4/5/6 down Lexington — no transfer, but Lex trains run crushed at rush.
